notes: Docket briefing items use letter IDs (A, B, C...) and represent pre-meeting briefing topics
Transcript agenda items use numeric IDs (100, 200, S400...) from the actual council meeting agenda
These are complementary but different numbering systems
Discussion time estimates are approximate, based on paragraph timing around item mentions
Keyword matching for docket-to-transcript correlation may miss items discussed under different terminology
key_discussion_points contains the top 3 most substantive points from the transcript discussion of each docket item, identified by keyword relevance scoring and filtered for procedural language

The Coastal Resilience Master Plan identifies potential nature based solutions for locations along San Diego's coast.

The Coastal Resilience Master Plan looks to achieve the following objectives: address the effects of sea level rise and coastal flooding while leveraging additional co benefits of nature based solutions, protect and enhance critical coastal habitat and associated wildlife from the impacts of climate change, protect and enhance recreation opportunities, protect historical, archaeological, and tribal cultural resources and incorporate indigenous knowledge into resilience efforts and adaptation strategies, and finally, increase coastal access for all community members with a prioritization of communities of concern.
